title: myxopyrum nervosum subsp. coriaceum
intro: Myxopyrum nervosum subsp. coriaceum, also known as the tough-leaved Myxopyrum, is a plant species in the family Moraceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Myxopyrum nervosum subsp. coriaceum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used as tea, or made into capsules or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
